I'll do a little bit of expanded background for me, just so that you can get a sense for where I'm coming from.
So I started my startup career as a founder in the insurtech space.
And I actually, I was a lawyer.
I left to go run a startup with a couple other folks.
It was backed by Warburg, Pincus, and Onyx.
And the idea was, can we build an online insurance marketplace, private labeled, for banks and financial institutions to sell insurance to their own customers under their brand name?
They have these great, they have these massive customer bases, think like Ford, Citibank, but they don't have like a digital offering for them.
So we built the online front end, we built the relationships with all the carriers across the US, and built this sort of competitive market.
Place where you can get comparative quotes.
We built marketing programs.
Ford Credit was the biggest customer that I signed, and we were their exclusive provider in the U.S.
So they got all the red truck, you know, marketing pieces online.
They would call up or they would either come to call center online, get a competitive quote.

Then I built a call center in Tennessee, staffed with 350 licensed agents to close all the business that came off the web and did that for Schwab, did that for Capital One, did that for Citibank, did that for Sears, sold the business to Capital One Bank in 2005.
So ran it from like, basically, I gave up my law practice and joined them.
After I negotiated my equity package with Onyx and everyone, the market crashed a month later.
So I built all this during the biggest headwinds of the dot-com crisis, which also was like an insane education.
Backable timing.

But then I had this idea that if I built a network of creatives, we were starting to get into the digital creatives space or era, I thought like if I can aggregate all of these creatives, designers, art directors, creative directors, illustrators, writers, photographers, and build a critical mass of them online where they can show their work and then market it to ad agencies and publishers.
The higher off the site, I thought that was like a really compelling model because sort of the creative.

And, and then I was there on an earn out and scaled that business.

then these like 12, 13 year old, like black hat affiliates that were generating, building blogs and promoting these offers in a very brand unsafe way.

So I built this agency, it's called Fusion20.com, and it was just like a website, this is like, you know, first year in college, and then I was like, how do I get customers so I don't have to go work in a restaurant, because I was horrible at it.

so I took my way through college doing that, which is a lot of fun.
